pi_items.source: Task description explicitly requires saving to this path. Confirmed in setup_task.sh which removes this file to ensure clean state (line 11), and in setup_freecad.sh which creates /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD directory (line 50).

Calculated from profile geometry: Lower cylinder (r=15mm, h=25mm) = 5625π ≈ 17671.46 mm³ + Upper cylinder (r=8mm, h=35mm) = 2240π ≈ 7037.17 mm³ = 7865π ≈ 24708.63 mm³. Metadata value differs by 6.63 mm³ (0.027%), well within tolerance.
Explicitly stated in task.json metadata (line 19). This is a standard tolerance for geometric calculations in CAD systems. The actual volume discrepancy is only 0.027%, far exceeding this tolerance.
Task description specifies lower section has 30mm diameter. Upper section has 16mm diameter. Maximum X-dimension (diameter) is 30mm. Bbox is [30, 30, 60] sorted by dimension magnitude.
Task creates a rotationally symmetric stepped cylinder (revolved profile). Y-dimension equals X-dimension (both are 30mm diameter) for a cylinder.
Task description explicitly states total height is 60mm (lower section 25mm + upper section 35mm = 60mm, lines 3 and 7 of description).
Explicitly stated in task.json metadata (line 21). This is a reasonable tolerance (5% of 60mm height, 10% of 30mm diameter) for CAD dimension verification.
Task description explicitly states 'lower section (30mm diameter, 25mm height)' (line 7). Profile coordinates: (0,0)→(15,0)→(15,25) creates radius 15mm = 30mm diameter.
Task description explicitly states 'lower section (30mm diameter, 25mm height)' (line 7). Profile coordinates (15,0)→(15,25) creates height 25mm.
Task description explicitly states 'upper section (16mm diameter, 35mm height)' (line 7). Profile coordinates: (8,25)→(8,60) creates radius 8mm = 16mm diameter.
Task description explicitly states 'upper section (16mm diameter, 35mm height)' (line 7). Verified by: total height 60mm - lower height 25mm = 35mm.
Task description states 'The final part should have a lower section (30mm diameter, 25mm height) and an upper section (16mm diameter, 35mm height).' Sum: 25mm + 35mm = 60mm.
Task description explicitly requires 'Create a sketch on the XZ plane' (line 2 of task description). This is the standard plane for profile sketches that will be revolved around the Z-axis.

Task description explicitly requires 'revolve this profile 360° around the vertical Z axis' (line 4 of task description). Verified in FreeCAD documentation that 360° creates a complete solid of revolution.
Task description states 'revolve this profile 360° around the vertical Z axis' (line 4 of task description). Z-axis is standard vertical axis in FreeCAD coordinate system.
Task description explicitly provides these coordinates (line 3 of task description). These coordinates in XZ plane create the stepped profile when revolved 360°.
Output file is stepped_shaft.FCStd. Verified in FreeCAD documentation that FCStd is the standard FreeCAD file format (a ZIP archive containing Document.xml with geometry and properties).
Task description explicitly states all coordinates and dimensions are in 'mm' (millimeters). This is confirmed as FreeCAD's default unit system.

Task description states to 'Use the Revolution tool'. Confirmed in setup_freecad.sh (line 26) which sets AutoloadModule to 'PartWorkbench', and export_result.sh uses Part workbench geometry inspection.

privileged_info_summary: The task requires creating a stepped shaft in FreeCAD by drawing a closed 2D profile on the XZ plane and revolving it 360° around the Z-axis. The profile has coordinates: (0,0)→(15,0)→(15,25)→(8,25)→(8,60)→(0,60)→back to (0,0) mm. This creates a stepped cylinder with: lower section (30mm diameter, 25mm height) and upper section (16mm diameter, 35mm height), totaling 60mm height. The theoretical volume is approximately 24,708.63 mm³ (task claims 24,702.0 mm³, difference 0.027%). The bounding box dimensions are [30, 30, 60] mm (sorted). The completed model must be saved as /home/ga/Documents/FreeCAD/stepped_shaft.FCStd in FreeCAD's native FCStd format (a ZIP archive containing geometry data). This is a synthetic training exercise testing CAD modeling fundamentals: sketch creation, profile definition, revolve operations, and file saving.
